body.title: [sprint] Strip OR/Anthropic cost rules; keep + reframe inference-proxy-only
body.intention: I intend that the dead OR-dollar rules are gone while the inference-proxy-only architecture rule stays (reframed as routing, not cost).
body.description:
**Problem:** many rules were about not burning OR budget; OR is fully disabled, so they're dead weight. But the 'inference-proxy-only' rule is the SPINE (route everything through the proxy) — keep it, reframed as routing not cost. New cost gating is now general (T-inference-usage-cost-gate), so the OR-specific rules can go.

**Shape:** Remove OR/Anthropic COST rules: palace unseenuniversity/rules/budget, day-close-audit Step 8 (OR burn rate — also a stale wild_igor path), OR-cost language in palace cc-minion/igor-constraints. KEEP + reframe inference-proxy-only. UPDATE (do NOT delete) the cost memories (project_ds_cost_constraint et al.) to the new state: 'satisfied — free inference (ollama_cloud/google_free) live; DS unblocked' — preserve the why.

**Affected files:** palace unseenuniversity/rules/budget + cc-minion + igor-constraints (DB), skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (Step 8), ~/.claude memory cost files (update), CLAUDE.md (only if it carries OR-cost rules; inference-proxy-only stays).
**Design rules:** keep inference-proxy-only (routing not cost); strip OR-dollar rules; preserve the why on updated memories.
**Scope boundary:** IN — remove OR cost rules; reframe-keep inference-proxy-only; UPDATE cost memories to satisfied-state. OUT — deleting inference-proxy-only; the router/gate (done in T-inference-policy-router / T-inference-usage-cost-gate).
**Test plan:** no tests because rules/docs/memory edits; verify inference-proxy-only still present (reframed); no OR-balance rule remains; project_ds_cost_constraint updated (not deleted) with why preserved.
**Completion criteria:** palace rules/budget + day-close Step 8 OR-burn removed; inference-proxy-only present and reframed (routing); project_ds_cost_constraint updated to satisfied-state (DS unblocked) with the why intact; `git grep`/palace search for OR-budget rules returns only historical/reframed mentions.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
